blurb: Protects the effective management and conservation of B.C.'s water, land, air and living resources — environmental assessments of major projects, waste and pollution permitting, contaminated site remediation, air quality, natural resource law enforcement, and the BC Parks system.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: British Columbians enjoy safe, well-managed parks and protected areas
goals.description: BC Parks manages camping, day-use and backcountry access across the provincial parks system and issues permits for commercial and film activity in parks.

supporting.name: Reserve a provincial park campsite
supporting.desc: Book frontcountry campsites, group camping and picnic shelters at BC Parks campgrounds across the province through the online camping reservation service.

legislation.full_name: Park Act  -  Issuing permits (s. 20)

supporting.name: Reserve a backcountry campsite, shelter or canoe circuit
supporting.desc: Book backcountry campgrounds, shelters and canoe circuits, or register for a backcountry permit in wilderness areas with no facilities.

legislation.full_name: Park Act  -  Issuing permits (s. 20)

supporting.name: Get a day-use pass for a busy provincial park
supporting.desc: Free timed day-use passes are required to visit Joffre Lakes, Garibaldi, Golden Ears in summer and Mount Seymour in winter to manage capacity and protect the visitor and conservation experience.

legislation.full_name: Park Act  -  Occupancy and use of land restricted (s. 16)

supporting.name: Cancel or get a refund for a park reservation
supporting.desc: Cancel a camping reservation online or through the call centre, and apply for a refund of eligible camping fees for closures, medical emergencies, poor air quality or other qualifying circumstances.

supporting.name: Find a park and check advisories before you go
supporting.desc: Search for a park by name or location and check current flood, wildfire and other advisories before visiting.

main_services.name: Get authorization to operate or hold an event in a park

supporting.name: Apply for a park use permit
supporting.desc: BC Parks regulates land use and occupancy of provincial parks and requires a park use permit for many commercial and non-recreational activities.

legislation.full_name: Park Act  -  Issuing permits (s. 20)

supporting.name: Apply for a commercial filming permit in a provincial park
supporting.desc: Film and production companies need a commercial filming permit, applied for through FrontCounter BC, to shoot in provincial parks and protected areas.

legislation.full_name: Park Act  -  Film production (s. 9.2)

supporting.name: Get a travel trade permit to lead commercial tours in parks
supporting.desc: Tour operators need a park use permit to guide commercial tour groups in provincial parks.

legislation.full_name: Park Act  -  Issuing permits (s. 20)

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: B.C.'s air, land and water are protected from pollution and contamination
goals.description: The ministry authorizes and regulates the discharge of waste to the environment, manages contaminated sites, oversees recycling and pesticide use, and responds to spills.

main_services.name: Get authorization to discharge waste to the environment

supporting.name: Apply for a waste discharge permit or approval
supporting.desc: A waste discharge authorization is written permission, required under the Environmental Management Act, to release air contaminants, effluent, garbage or hazardous waste into the environment.

legislation.full_name: Environmental Management Act  -  Waste disposal authorizations (s. 6)

relationships.service: Report a poacher, polluter or environmental violation (RAPP)

supporting.name: Check the status of a waste discharge authorization application
supporting.desc: Search for existing waste discharge authorizations and check the progress of an application under review.

legislation.full_name: Environmental Management Act  -  Waste disposal authorizations (s. 6)

supporting.name: Comply with and report under a waste discharge authorization
supporting.desc: Authorization holders must submit monitoring reports and data, prepare for inspections, and report non-compliance to the ministry.

legislation.full_name: Environmental Management Act  -  Compliance and enforcement

relationships.service: Report a spill or environmental emergency

main_services.name: Investigate and remediate a contaminated site

supporting.name: Identify and disclose a potentially contaminated site
supporting.desc: Property owners, buyers and consultants can identify a site that may be contaminated and file a site disclosure statement where required.

legislation.full_name: Environmental Management Act  -  Site disclosure statements (s. 40)

supporting.name: Apply for contaminated site remediation services
supporting.desc: Submit a Contaminated Site Services Application, notification of independent remediation, or related forms to investigate, classify and remediate a contaminated site.

legislation.full_name: Environmental Management Act  -  Contaminated site remediation (Part 4)

supporting.name: Get a certificate of compliance for a remediated site
supporting.desc: Site owners can obtain an approval in principle or certificate of compliance confirming a contaminated site has been remediated to required standards.

legislation.full_name: Environmental Management Act  -  Approvals in principle and certificates of compliance (s. 53)

supporting.name: Report a spill or environmental emergency
supporting.desc: Anyone who causes or discovers a spill that threatens water, land or air quality must report it immediately to the province's 24-hour Environmental Emergency reporting line.

legislation.full_name: Environmental Management Act  -  Spill prevention and reporting (s. 79)

relationships.service: Comply with and report under a waste discharge authorization

supporting.name: Report a poacher, polluter or environmental violation (RAPP)
supporting.desc: The Report All Poachers and Polluters (RAPP) tip line and online form lets anyone anonymously report known or suspected violations of fisheries, wildlife or environmental protection laws to the Conservation Officer Service.

legislation.full_name: Environmental Management Act  -  Conservation Officer Service (Part 9)

relationships.service: Apply for a waste discharge permit or approval

supporting.name: Report a human-wildlife conflict or dangerous wildlife encounter
supporting.desc: Report bear, cougar and other wildlife encounters that may pose a public safety risk to the Conservation Officer Service through the RAPP line.

supporting.name: Find where to recycle a product
supporting.desc: Look up recycling locations, accepted products, deposits and refunds under B.C.'s Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) system.

legislation.full_name: Environmental Management Act  -  Recycling Regulation

relationships.service: Develop and file an extended producer responsibility stewardship plan

supporting.name: Develop and file an extended producer responsibility stewardship plan
supporting.desc: Producers of regulated products (packaging, electronics, tires and more) must develop and submit an EPR stewardship plan and annual reports under the Recycling Regulation.
supporting.users: Producers and brand owners of regulated products

channels.label: Producer responsibilities and EPR plan development
Approved EPR plans and annual reports

legislation.full_name: Environmental Management Act  -  Recycling Regulation

relationships.service: Find where to recycle a product

main_services.name: Sell, use or manage pesticides safely

supporting.name: Get certified to sell or use pesticides
supporting.desc: Only a trained and certified person may sell or use most pesticides in B.C.; certification requires study, an exam, and continuing education credits.

legislation.full_name: Integrated Pest Management Act  -  Certificate for sale or use of pesticides (s. 5)

supporting.name: Apply for a permit to use pesticides commercially
supporting.desc: Businesses using pesticides for pest management activities may require a permit or licence under the Integrated Pest Management Act.

legislation.full_name: Integrated Pest Management Act  -  Licence and permit for pesticide use (ss. 4, 6)

supporting.name: Report a pesticide-related concern
supporting.desc: Report suspected misuse of pesticides or non-compliance with the Integrated Pest Management Act and Regulation.

legislation.full_name: Integrated Pest Management Act  -  Compliance (Part 5)

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: Major projects undergo rigorous, efficient environmental assessment
goals.description: The Environmental Assessment Office assesses the potential environmental, economic, social, cultural and health effects of major projects proposed in B.C., in consultation with First Nations, communities and agencies.

main_services.name: Get an environmental assessment for a major project

supporting.name: Apply for an environmental assessment certificate
supporting.desc: Major projects (mines, major industrial facilities, large energy and infrastructure projects) must undergo an environmental assessment led by the Environmental Assessment Office before proceeding.

legislation.full_name: Environmental Assessment Act  -  Assessment of reviewable projects

relationships.service: Check the status of a project under environmental assessment

supporting.name: Check the status of a project under environmental assessment
supporting.desc: The EAO's Project Information Centre (EPIC) publishes documents and status information for projects undergoing environmental assessment.
supporting.users: General public, First Nations, local governments, proponents

legislation.full_name: Environmental Assessment Act  -  Assessment of reviewable projects

relationships.service: Apply for an environmental assessment certificate

supporting.name: Comment on a project undergoing environmental assessment
supporting.desc: Members of the public can submit comments during designated comment periods to help ensure community values and potential effects are considered in an assessment.

legislation.full_name: Environmental Assessment Act  -  Public and Indigenous participation

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: B.C.'s air quality is monitored and natural resource laws are enforced
goals.description: The ministry monitors and manages air quality across the province and the Conservation Officer Service enforces fisheries, wildlife and environmental protection laws.

main_services.name: Check air quality before you go outside

supporting.name: Check current air quality and health index readings
supporting.desc: Look up current air quality data, the Air Quality Health Index, and active air quality advisories for communities across B.C.

legislation.full_name: Environmental Management Act  -  Control of air contaminants (s. 72)

relationships.service: Get a permit to conduct open burning

supporting.name: Get a permit to conduct open burning
supporting.desc: Check the ventilation index and burning restrictions, and get authorization where required, before conducting open burning of land-clearing or agricultural debris.
supporting.users: Land owners, land clearing operators, agricultural producers

legislation.full_name: Environmental Management Act  -  Burning regulations (s. 76)

relationships.service: Check current air quality and health index readings
Check current campfire bans and fire restrictions

main_services.name: Get help with human-wildlife conflict and natural resource enforcement

supporting.name: Learn how to prevent human-wildlife conflict
supporting.desc: Get guidance on staying safe around wildlife such as bears and cougars, and on preventing conflicts that put people, pets and animals at risk.

supporting.name: Contact the Conservation Officer Service
supporting.desc: Contact the BC Conservation Officer Service for information, to follow up on a report, or for career and general inquiries.

legislation.full_name: Environmental Management Act  -  Conservation Officer Service (Part 9)

foundational_services.name: Air, water and environmental quality monitoring and reporting
foundational_services.desc: Scientific monitoring, modelling and reporting on air, water and environmental quality that underlies public-facing advisories, standards and permitting decisions.

foundational_services.name: Natural resource law enforcement operations
foundational_services.desc: Field enforcement, investigations and prosecutions carried out by the Conservation Officer Service in response to reported and detected violations.

foundational_services.name: Provincial parks and protected areas planning and operations
foundational_services.desc: Park boundary, classification, conservation and infrastructure planning that underlies BC Parks' visitor-facing camping, permitting and conservation programs.
